markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive have locked in a November 19, 2026 release for GTA VI on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S following two delays from the original Fall 2025 target. This firm date, reaffirmed in recent earnings calls, anchors trader sentiment around the game's proximity to launch. Marketing momentum is building with expectations for a third trailer, pre-order openings, and a summer campaign kickoff in late June 2026, which could trigger related market resolutions. The novelty of the market—pitting the blockbuster title against unlikely or satirical outcomes—draws attention to how confirmed industry timelines and upcoming promotional events shape consensus, even as entertainment releases remain subject to last-minute shifts.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Harvey Weinstein prison time?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Strong trader consensus for no additional prison time (80.8%) stems primarily from repeated New York mistrials and time already served.** Weinstein’s original 2020 New York conviction was overturned on appeal in 2024, and subsequent retrials produced only a single conviction on one count in 2025 before juries deadlocked again, most recently declaring a mistrial on May 15, 2026, in the remaining rape charge tied to Jessica Mann. Prosecutors now face a June 24 hearing on whether to pursue yet another retrial, creating uncertainty and raising prospects for plea deals, concurrent sentencing, or dismissal that would limit further incarceration.

His existing 16-year California sentence from the 2022 conviction, combined with roughly six years served since 2020 and documented health issues at age 74, reinforces market-implied odds that any new penalties will not materially extend his time behind bars. The low probabilities attached to longer sentences (20–30 years at 8.1%, others under 3%) reflect the cumulative effect of hung juries, appeals momentum, and practical constraints on consecutive terms in the #MeToo-era cases. Upcoming court decisions remain the key near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold an 79.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Stanley Cup after taking a 3-2 series lead in the Final against the Vegas Golden Knights, driven by back-to-back victories including a 4-2 Game 5 win on June 11 powered by multi-goal efforts from Andrei Svechnikov and Sebastian Aho. The Canes posted the NHL's best regular-season record in the East with 113 points and advanced through the bracket with strong defensive structure and depth. Vegas, at 20%, retains realistic upset chances in Game 6 on the road or a potential Game 7 at home but faces elimination pressure after dropping the last two contests following an earlier 2-1 series advantage. Recent playoff momentum and home-ice factors in the remaining games shape current trader consensus on the outcome.

The Canes posted the NHL's best regular-season record in the East with 113 points and advanced through the bracket with strong defensive structure and depth. Vegas, at 20%, retains realistic upset chances in Game 6 on the road or a potential Game 7 at home but faces elimination pressure after dropping the last two contests following an earlier 2-1 series advantage. Recent playoff momentum and home-ice factors in the remaining games shape current trader consensus on the outcome.

markets.question: Will the New York Knicks win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the New York Knicks win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold an 80.8% implied probability in the 2026 NBA Championship market after taking a 3-1 lead in the Finals against the San Antonio Spurs. Their position stems from a record 29-point comeback victory in Game 4, sealed by OG Anunoby’s tip-in with 1.2 seconds left, following strong contributions from Jalen Brunson. The Spurs, at 19.1%, advanced as the Western Conference’s top seed behind Victor Wembanyama but have dropped three straight after early series dominance. Trader consensus prices the Knicks’ path to their first title since 1973 as heavily favored heading into Game 5 in San Antonio, reflecting recent playoff momentum and series history over regular-season records.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the 2026 World Cup winner market at similar implied probabilities due to their elite squad depth, recent international success including Spain’s Euro 2024 title, and strong pre-tournament form in friendlies. Portugal and England sit just behind, buoyed by Cristiano Ronaldo’s experience alongside rising talents and England’s consistent major-tournament pedigree under Thomas Tuchel. The bunched top tier reflects high overall parity among established powers in an expanded 48-team field, where group-stage results and knockout upsets can quickly reshape paths to the July final.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's consolidated authority within the Chinese Communist Party, including loyalists dominating the Politburo Standing Committee and military leadership, underpins the strong trader consensus against his removal before 2027. He has not designated a successor, a deliberate approach that avoids creating rival power centers while enabling expectations of a fourth term at the 21st Party Congress in 2027. Recent analyses highlight the lack of visible internal divisions or factional challenges capable of forcing an exit, despite ongoing personnel shifts and nominal age norms that have become flexible under his rule. Broader factors such as extensive surveillance mechanisms and party discipline further reduce the odds of abrupt leadership change within the resolution window.
